dsql.description: Region where the resource(s) will be managed. Defaults to the Region set in the provider configuration
dsql.name: region

dsql.description: A map of tags to add to all resources
dsql.name: tags

dsql.description: Whether deletion protection is enabled in this cluster
dsql.name: deletion_protection_enabled

dsql.description: The ARN of the AWS KMS key that encrypts data in the DSQL Cluster, or `AWS_OWNED_KMS_KEY`
dsql.name: kms_encryption_key

dsql.description: Witness region for the multi-region clusters. Setting this makes this cluster a multi-region cluster. Changing it recreates the cluster
dsql.name: witness_region

dsql.description: Whether cluster should be created (affects all resources)
dsql.name: create

dsql.description: Destroys cluster even if deletion_protection_enabled is set to true. Default value is false
dsql.name: force_destroy

dsql.description: List of DSQL Cluster ARNs to be peered to this cluster
dsql.name: clusters

root.description: Specifies whether or not mappings of AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) accounts to database accounts is enabled
root.name: iam_database_authentication_enabled

root.description: IAM role used by RDS to send enhanced monitoring metrics to CloudWatch
root.name: monitoring_role_arn

root.description: CPU threshold which will initiate autoscaling
root.name: autoscaling_target_cpu

root.description: The length of the rotation window in hours. For example, 3h for a three hour window
root.name: master_user_password_rotation_duration

root.description: Instance parameter group to associate with all instances of the DB cluster. The `cluster_db_instance_parameter_group_name` is only valid in combination with `allow_major_version_upgrade`
root.name: cluster_db_instance_parameter_group_name

root.description: The source region for an encrypted replica DB cluster
root.name: source_region

root.description: Create, update, and delete timeout configurations for the cluster instance(s)
root.name: instance_timeouts

root.description: Determines whether a CloudWatch log group is created for each `enabled_cloudwatch_logs_exports`
root.name: create_cloudwatch_log_group

root.description: The compute and memory capacity of each DB instance in the Multi-AZ DB cluster (not all DB instance classes are available in all AWS Regions, or for all database engines)
root.name: cluster_instance_class

root.description: The days to retain backups for
root.name: backup_retention_period

root.description: Specifies the amount of time to retain performance insights data for. Defaults to 7 days if Performance Insights are enabled. Valid values are 7, month * 31 (where month is a number of months from 1-23), and 731
root.name: cluster_performance_insights_retention_period

root.description: Whether to use `name` as a prefix for the cluster
root.name: cluster_use_name_prefix

root.description: Specifies whether or not to create this cluster from a snapshot. You can use either the name or ARN when specifying a DB cluster snapshot, or the ARN when specifying a DB snapshot
root.name: snapshot_identifier

root.description: Cooldown in seconds before allowing further scaling operations after a scale out
root.name: autoscaling_scale_out_cooldown

root.description: ID of the VPC where to create security group
root.name: vpc_id

root.description: If the DB instance should have deletion protection enabled. The database can't be deleted when this value is set to `true`. The default is `false`
root.name: deletion_protection

root.description: The ARN for the KMS encryption key. When specifying `kms_key_id`, `storage_encrypted` needs to be set to `true`
root.name: kms_key_id

root.description: Daily time range during which automated backups are created if automated backups are enabled using the BackupRetentionPeriod parameter.Time in UTC. Default: A 30-minute window selected at random from an 8-hour block of time per region, e.g. `04:00-09:00`
root.name: preferred_backup_window

root.description: Weekly time range during which system maintenance can occur, in (UTC) e.g., `wed:04:00-wed:04:30`
root.name: preferred_maintenance_window

root.description: Determines whether autoscaling of the cluster read replicas is enabled
root.name: autoscaling_enabled

root.description: Specified the log class of the log group. Possible values are: STANDARD or INFREQUENT_ACCESS
root.name: cloudwatch_log_group_class

root.description: Copy all Cluster `tags` to snapshots
root.name: copy_tags_to_snapshot

root.description: Name for an automatically created database on cluster creation
root.name: database_name

root.description: A map of tags to add to only the cluster. Used for AWS Instance Scheduler tagging
root.name: cluster_tags

root.description: The life cycle type for this DB instance. This setting is valid for cluster types Aurora DB clusters and Multi-AZ DB clusters. Valid values are `open-source-rds-extended-support`, `open-source-rds-extended-support-disabled`. Default value is `open-source-rds-extended-support`
root.name: engine_lifecycle_support

root.description: The amount of Provisioned IOPS (input/output operations per second) to be initially allocated for each DB instance in the Multi-AZ DB cluster
root.name: iops

root.description: Write-Only required unless `manage_master_user_password` is set to `true`, a `snapshot_identifier`, `replication_source_identifier`, or unless a `global_cluster_identifier` is provided when the cluster is the "secondary" cluster of a global database) Password for the master DB user
root.name: master_password_wo

root.description: The type of network stack to use (IPV4 or DUAL)
root.name: network_type

root.description: Map of cluster instances and any specific/overriding attributes to be created
root.name: instances
root.type:
map(object({
    apply_immediately                     = optional(bool)
    auto_minor_version_upgrade            = optional(bool)
    availability_zone                     = optional(string)
    ca_cert_identifier                    = optional(string)
    copy_tags_to_snapshot                 = optional(bool, true)
    custom_iam_instance_profile           = optional(string)
    db_parameter_group_name               = optional(string)
    db_subnet_group_name                  = optional(string)
    identifier                            = optional(string)
    identifier_prefix                     = optional(string)
    instance_class                        = optional(string)
    monitoring_interval                   = optional(number)
    monitoring_role_arn                   = optional(string)
    performance_insights_enabled          = optional(bool)
    performance_insights_kms_key_id       = optional(string)
    performance_insights_retention_period = optional(number)
    preferred_maintenance_window          = optional(string)
    promotion_tier                        = optional(number)
    publicly_accessible                   = optional(bool)
    tags                                  = optional(map(string), {})
  }))

root.description: Map of arguments for the created DB cluster activity stream
root.name: cluster_activity_stream

root.description: Whether cluster should forward writes to an associated global cluster. Applied to secondary clusters to enable them to forward writes to an `aws_rds_global_cluster`'s primary cluster
root.name: enable_global_write_forwarding

root.description: Name used across resources created
root.name: name

root.description: Specifies whether any cluster modifications are applied immediately, or during the next maintenance window. Default is `false`
root.name: apply_immediately

root.description: List of RDS Instances that are a part of this cluster
root.name: cluster_members

root.description: The database engine version. Updating this argument results in an outage
root.name: engine_version

root.description: The name of an existing DB cluster parameter group. Required when `cluster_parameter_group` is not provided (`null`)
root.name: cluster_parameter_group_name

root.description: Map of nested attributes for cloning Aurora cluster
root.name: restore_to_point_in_time
root.type:
object({
    restore_to_time            = optional(string)
    restore_type               = optional(string)
    source_cluster_identifier  = optional(string)
    source_cluster_resource_id = optional(string)
    use_latest_restorable_time = optional(bool)
  })

root.description: Determines whether cluster is primary cluster with writer instance (set to `false` for global cluster and replica clusters)
root.name: is_primary_cluster

root.description: Enable HTTP endpoint (data API). Only valid when engine_mode is set to `serverless`
root.name: enable_http_endpoint

root.description: Configuration map used to restore from a Percona Xtrabackup in S3 (only MySQL is supported)
root.name: s3_import

root.description: Arguments for the DB shard group to be created
root.name: shard_group

root.description: Determines whether to create the database subnet group or use existing
root.name: create_db_subnet_group

root.description: Map of nested arguments for the created DB parameter group
root.name: db_parameter_group

root.description: Set to true if you do not wish the log group (and any logs it may contain) to be deleted at destroy time, and instead just remove the log group from the Terraform state
root.name: cloudwatch_log_group_skip_destroy

root.description: Map of additional cluster endpoints and their attributes to be created
root.name: endpoints
root.type:
map(object({
    identifier       = string
    type             = string
    excluded_members = optional(list(string))
    static_members   = optional(list(string))
    tags             = optional(map(string), {})
  }))

root.description: Description of the monitoring role
root.name: iam_role_description

root.description: Map of nested arguments for the created DB cluster parameter group
root.name: cluster_parameter_group

root.description: The number of days to retain CloudWatch logs for the DB instance
root.name: cloudwatch_log_group_retention_in_days

root.description: List of EC2 Availability Zones for the DB cluster storage where DB cluster instances can be created. RDS automatically assigns 3 AZs if less than 3 AZs are configured, which will show as a difference requiring resource recreation next Terraform apply
root.name: availability_zones

root.description: Determines whether a final snapshot is created before the cluster is deleted. If true is specified, no snapshot is created
root.name: skip_final_snapshot

root.description: Additional tags for the CloudWatch log group(s)
root.name: cloudwatch_log_group_tags

root.description: The port on which the DB accepts connections
root.name: port

root.description: The security group name. Default value is (`var.name`)
root.name: security_group_name

root.description: A cron() or rate() expression that defines the schedule for rotating your secret. Either `master_user_password_rotation_automatically_after_days` or `master_user_password_rotation_schedule_expression` must be specified
root.name: master_user_password_rotation_schedule_expression

root.description: Determines whether cluster instance identifiers are used as prefixes
root.name: instances_use_identifier_prefix

root.description: The Amazon Web Services KMS key identifier is the key ARN, key ID, alias ARN, or alias name for the KMS key
root.name: master_user_secret_kms_key_id

root.description: The name of the subnet group name (existing or created)
root.name: db_subnet_group_name

root.description: List of subnet IDs used by database subnet group created
root.name: subnets

root.description: Interval, in seconds, between points when Enhanced Monitoring metrics are collected for the DB cluster. To turn off collecting Enhanced Monitoring metrics, specify 0. Valid Values: 0, 1, 5, 10, 15, 30, 60
root.name: cluster_monitoring_interval

root.description: The name of the database engine to be used for this DB cluster. Defaults to `aurora`. Valid Values: `aurora`, `aurora-mysql`, `aurora-postgresql`
root.name: engine

root.description: Username for the master DB user. Required unless `snapshot_identifier` or `replication_source_identifier` is provided or unless a `global_cluster_identifier` is provided when the cluster is the secondary cluster of a global database
root.name: master_username

root.description: The ARN of the KMS Key to use when encrypting log data
root.name: cloudwatch_log_group_kms_key_id

root.description: Specifies whether to remove automated backups immediately after the DB cluster is deleted
root.name: delete_automated_backups

root.description: Maximum number of read replicas permitted when autoscaling is enabled
root.name: autoscaling_max_capacity

root.description: Whether cluster should be created (affects nearly all resources)
root.name: create

root.description: The ARN of the policy that is used to set the permissions boundary for the monitoring role
root.name: iam_role_permissions_boundary

root.description: The metric type to scale on. Valid values are `RDSReaderAverageCPUUtilization` and `RDSReaderAverageDatabaseConnections`
root.name: predefined_metric_type

root.description: Average number of connections threshold which will initiate autoscaling. Default value is 70% of db.r4/r5/r6g.large's default max_connections
root.name: autoscaling_target_connections

root.description: Do you agree that Putin doesn't respect Ukrainian sovereignty and territorial integrity? More info: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Putin_khuylo!
root.name: putin_khuylo

root.description: ARN of a source DB cluster or DB instance if this DB cluster is to be created as a Read Replica
root.name: replication_source_identifier

root.description: The ID of the Directory Service Active Directory domain to create the instance in
root.name: domain

root.description: Set to true to allow RDS to manage the master user password in Secrets Manager. Cannot be set if `master_password_wo` is provided
root.name: manage_master_user_password

root.description: Determines whether the security group name (`var.name`) is used as a prefix
root.name: security_group_use_name_prefix

root.description: The target backtrack window, in seconds. Only available for `aurora` engine currently. To disable backtracking, set this value to 0. Must be between 0 and 259200 (72 hours)
root.name: backtrack_window

root.description: The mode of Database Insights to enable for the DB cluster. Valid values: standard, advanced
root.name: database_insights_mode

root.description: Whether read replicas can forward write operations to the writer DB instance in the DB cluster. By default, write operations aren't allowed on reader DB instances
root.name: enable_local_write_forwarding

root.description: Set of log types to export to cloudwatch. If omitted, no logs will be exported. The following log types are supported: `audit`, `error`, `general`, `slowquery`, `postgresql`
root.name: enabled_cloudwatch_logs_exports

root.description: Map of nested attributes with serverless v2 scaling properties. Only valid when `engine_mode` is set to `provisioned`
root.name: serverlessv2_scaling_configuration

root.description: Autoscaling policy name
root.name: autoscaling_policy_name

root.description: Specifies the scalability mode of the Aurora DB cluster. When set to limitless, the cluster operates as an Aurora Limitless Database. When set to standard (the default), the cluster uses normal DB instance creation. Valid values: limitless, standard
root.name: cluster_scalability_type

root.description: The name of your final DB snapshot when this DB cluster is deleted. If omitted, no final snapshot will be made
root.name: final_snapshot_identifier

root.description: The global cluster identifier specified on `aws_rds_global_cluster`
root.name: global_cluster_identifier

root.description: Minimum number of read replicas permitted when autoscaling is enabled
root.name: autoscaling_min_capacity

root.description: Cooldown in seconds before allowing further scaling operations after a scale in
root.name: autoscaling_scale_in_cooldown

root.description: Map of security group egress rules to add to the security group created
root.name: security_group_egress_rules
root.type:
map(object({
    name = optional(string)

    cidr_ipv4                    = optional(string)
    cidr_ipv6                    = optional(string)
    description                  = optional(string)
    from_port                    = optional(number)
    ip_protocol                  = optional(string, "tcp")
    prefix_list_id               = optional(string)
    referenced_security_group_id = optional(string)
    region                       = optional(string)
    tags                         = optional(map(string), {})
    to_port                      = optional(number)
  }))

root.description: Specifies the number of days between automatic scheduled rotations of the secret. Either `master_user_password_rotation_automatically_after_days` or `master_user_password_rotation_schedule_expression` must be specified
root.name: master_user_password_rotation_automatically_after_days

root.description: Specifies the KMS Key ID to encrypt Performance Insights data. If not specified, the default RDS KMS key will be used (aws/rds)
root.name: cluster_performance_insights_kms_key_id

root.description: Path for the monitoring role
root.name: iam_role_path

root.description: Maximum session duration (in seconds) that you want to set for the monitoring role
root.name: iam_role_max_session_duration

root.description: The description of the security group. If value is set to empty string it will contain cluster name in the description
root.name: security_group_description

root.description: Specifies whether to rotate the secret immediately or wait until the next scheduled rotation window
root.name: master_user_password_rotate_immediately

root.description: Enables Performance Insights for the RDS Cluster
root.name: cluster_performance_insights_enabled

root.description: Map of nested attributes with scaling properties. Only valid when `engine_mode` is set to `serverless`
root.name: scaling_configuration
root.type:
object({
    auto_pause               = optional(bool)
    max_capacity             = optional(number)
    min_capacity             = optional(number)
    seconds_before_timeout   = optional(number)
    seconds_until_auto_pause = optional(number)
    timeout_action           = optional(string)
  })

root.description: Determines whether to use `iam_role_name` as is or create a unique name beginning with the `iam_role_name` as the prefix
root.name: iam_role_use_name_prefix

root.description: Region where the resource(s) will be managed. Defaults to the Region set in the provider configuration
root.name: region

root.description: Used together with `master_password_wo` to trigger an update. Increment this value when an update to the `master_password_wo` is required
root.name: master_password_wo_version

root.description: Specifies whether the DB cluster is encrypted. The default is `true`
root.name: storage_encrypted

root.description: Determines whether to create security group for RDS cluster
root.name: create_security_group

root.description: A map of tags to add to all resources
root.name: tags

root.description: Whether to apply minor engine upgrades automatically to the DB cluster during the maintenance window. Defaults to `true`
root.name: auto_minor_version_upgrade

root.description: (Required if domain is provided) The name of the IAM role to be used when making API calls to the Directory Service
root.name: domain_iam_role_name

root.description: The database engine mode. Valid values: `global`, `multimaster`, `parallelquery`, `provisioned`, `serverless`. Defaults to: `provisioned`
root.name: engine_mode

root.description: The amount of storage in gibibytes (GiB) to allocate to each DB instance in the Multi-AZ DB cluster. (This setting is required to create a Multi-AZ DB cluster)
root.name: allocated_storage

root.description: Enable to allow major engine version upgrades when changing engine versions. Defaults to `false`
root.name: allow_major_version_upgrade

root.description: Map of IAM roles and supported feature names to associate with the cluster
root.name: role_associations

root.description: Determines whether to create the IAM role for RDS enhanced monitoring
root.name: create_monitoring_role

root.description: Map of security group ingress rules to add to the security group created
root.name: security_group_ingress_rules
root.type:
map(object({
    name = optional(string)

    cidr_ipv4                    = optional(string)
    cidr_ipv6                    = optional(string)
    description                  = optional(string)
    from_port                    = optional(number)
    ip_protocol                  = optional(string, "tcp")
    prefix_list_id               = optional(string)
    referenced_security_group_id = optional(string)
    region                       = optional(string)
    tags                         = optional(map(string), {})
    to_port                      = optional(number)
  }))

root.description: Additional tags for the security group
root.name: security_group_tags

root.description: Whether to manage the master user password rotation. By default, false on creation, rotation is managed by RDS. There is not currently a way to disable this on initial creation even when set to false. Setting this value to false after previously having been set to true will disable automatic rotation
root.name: manage_master_user_password_rotation

root.description: The CA certificate identifier to use for the DB cluster's server certificate. Currently only supported for multi-az DB clusters
root.name: cluster_ca_cert_identifier

root.description: List of VPC security groups to associate to the cluster in addition to the security group created
root.name: vpc_security_group_ids

root.description: Create, update, and delete timeout configurations for the cluster
root.name: cluster_timeouts

root.description: Friendly name of the monitoring role
root.name: iam_role_name

root.description: Determines the storage type for the DB cluster. Optional for Single-AZ, required for Multi-AZ DB clusters. Valid values for Single-AZ: `aurora`, `""` (default, both refer to Aurora Standard), `aurora-iopt1` (Aurora I/O Optimized). Valid values for Multi-AZ: `io1` (default)
root.name: storage_type

dsql.description: Encryption configuration details for the DSQL cluster
dsql.name: encryption_details

dsql.description: Multi-region properties of the DSQL cluster
dsql.name: multi_region_properties

dsql.description: The DSQL cluster's VPC endpoint service name
dsql.name: vpc_endpoint_service_name

root.description: The database port
root.name: cluster_port

root.description: The Route53 Hosted Zone ID of the endpoint
root.name: cluster_hosted_zone_id

root.description: A map of cluster instances and their attributes
root.name: cluster_instances

root.description: A map of IAM roles associated with the cluster and their attributes
root.name: cluster_role_associations

root.description: The ARN of the DB cluster parameter group created
root.name: db_cluster_parameter_group_arn

root.description: Map of CloudWatch log groups created and their attributes
root.name: db_cluster_cloudwatch_log_groups

root.description: The RDS Cluster Identifier
root.name: cluster_id

root.description: Order in which the clusters are upgraded (first, second, last)
root.name: cluster_upgrade_rollout_order

root.description: The name of the enhanced monitoring role
root.name: enhanced_monitoring_iam_role_name

root.description: The security group ID of the cluster
root.name: security_group_id

root.description: The ID of the DB cluster parameter group created
root.name: db_cluster_parameter_group_id

root.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of cluster
root.name: cluster_arn

root.description: Stable and unique string identifying the enhanced monitoring role
root.name: enhanced_monitoring_iam_role_unique_id

root.description: The ID of the DB parameter group created
root.name: db_parameter_group_id

root.description: The connection endpoint for the DB shard group
root.name: db_shard_group_endpoint

root.description: Specifies whether automatic rotation is enabled for the secret
root.name: db_cluster_secretsmanager_secret_rotation_enabled

root.description: List of RDS Instances that are a part of this cluster
root.name: cluster_members

root.description: Name for an automatically created database on cluster creation
root.name: cluster_database_name

root.description: CA identifier of the CA certificate used for the DB instance's server certificate
root.name: cluster_ca_certificate_identifier

root.description: The ARN of the DB parameter group created
root.name: db_parameter_group_arn

root.description: The db subnet group name
root.name: db_subnet_group_name

root.description: Writer endpoint for the cluster
root.name: cluster_endpoint

root.description: A read-only endpoint for the cluster, automatically load-balanced across replicas
root.name: cluster_reader_endpoint

root.description: ARN of the shard group
root.name: db_shard_group_arn

root.description: The AWS Region-unique, immutable identifier for the DB shard group
root.name: db_shard_group_resource_id

root.description: The RDS Cluster Resource ID
root.name: cluster_resource_id

root.description: The running version of the cluster database
root.name: cluster_engine_version_actual

root.description: The generated database master user secret when `manage_master_user_password` is set to `true`
root.name: cluster_master_user_secret

root.description: Expiration date of the DB instance’s server certificate
root.name: cluster_ca_certificate_valid_till

root.description: The name of the Amazon Kinesis data stream to be used for the database activity stream
root.name: db_cluster_activity_stream_kinesis_stream_name

root.description: The database master username
root.name: cluster_master_username

root.description: A map of additional cluster endpoints and their attributes
root.name: additional_cluster_endpoints

root.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) specifying the enhanced monitoring role
root.name: enhanced_monitoring_iam_role_arn
